sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The game is no longer over. Looking at the image, the player is in the center, and the frames are visible. Let me check the current state to understand what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current frames:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'b'] and n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nPlayer:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check what changed
if last_transition:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    before_b = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    after_b = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    print(f"\nBefore: {len(before_b)} 'b' frames")
    print(f"After: {len(after_b)} 'b' frames")
    
    before_g = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    after_g = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    print(f"Before: {len(before_g)} 'G' frames")
    print(f"After: {len(after_g)} 'G' frames")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player is at row 36-43, col 32-35 (white head at row 36-40, green body at row 41-43)
# Center frame (b) is at row 28-31, col 28-39
